channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🕵️ Preparing Australia for a future of overlapping risk | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Governments still organise around discrete shocks, yet the operating environment increasingly delivers continuous, concurrent and cascading pressures across economic, social, technological and environmental systems. Australia needs to shift from managing shocks to managing…

item.title: 🌏 Sorry, Mr Carney. At Shangri-la, Indo-Pacific countries backed the rules-based order | ASPI Strategist
item.description: International rules still matter. That was the common theme at this year’s Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore, particularly for Indo-Pacific nations. Australia’s deputy prime minister and defence minister, Richard Marles, told the conference, ‘The international rules-based ...

item.title: 🏴 Russian Trucks Get ‘Dazzle’ Paint To Throw Off AI-Enabled Drones | The War Zone
item.description: The disruptive paint appears designed to confuse machine-vision targeting systems as autonomous drones become a growing threat. The post Russian Trucks Get ‘Dazzle’ Paint To Throw Off AI-Enabled Drones appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Is The U.S. Flying MQ-1 Predator Drones Again? | The War Zone
item.description: Questions surrounding the loss of an "MQ-1" to Iran highlight the possibilities and pitfalls of sending the Predator back into the fray. The post Is The U.S. Flying MQ-1 Predator Drones Again? appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon chief sounds ‘alarm’ over China’s buildup, urges allies to boost defense spending | Defense News
item.description: Hegseth urged Asian allies to ramp up military spending to counter China’s growing power.

item.title: 🌍 U.S.-Iran Ceasefire On The Brink After Tit-For-Tat Attacks (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: Adding to the pressure, Iran has renewed threats to also close the Bab Al-Mandeb Strait, now an absolutely critical conduit for oil exports. The post U.S.-Iran Ceasefire On The Brink After Tit-For-Tat Attacks (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Trump Says Israel, Hezbollah to Halt Attacks as Iran Talks Continue | Foreign Policy
item.description: Tehran had said it would suspend negotiations over Israeli strikes in Lebanon.

item.title: 🌏 Suddenly, we hear of Australian–Japanese ‘strategic depth’. Here’s what it may mean | ASPI Strategist
item.description: A new element has just appeared in Australia–Japan defence and security cooperation: strategic depth. But the two countries, having mentioned it, haven’t gone far to explain what they mean by it. Nonetheless, some fairly safe ...

item.title: 🏛️ Ready, fire, aim: Pentagon cut workforce with little analysis before or since, GAO finds | Defense One
item.description: Defense officials concurred that lessons should be drawn—but gave no indication they will be.

item.title: 🏛️ The Navy wants next-generation munitions, so it’s spending millions on innovation hubs | Defense One
item.description: The service is teaming up with industrial park-builder ACMI to create an energetics facility in Maryland.

item.title: 📍 Experts react: Colombia’s presidential runoff pits the far right against the far left. What’s next? | Atlantic Council
item.description: Abelardo de la Espriella and Iván Cepeda, who represent opposite ends of the political spectrum, will move ahead to the June 21 runoff. The post Experts react: Colombia’s presidential runoff pits the far right against the far left. What’s next? appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 🌍 A Trump Deal With Iran Could Spell Trouble for Netanyahu | Foreign Policy
item.description: The Israeli leader needs a win ahead of a key election.

item.title: 🏛️ Littoral Combat Force Takes Up Station In Caribbean Under Navy’s New Deployment Concept | The War Zone
item.description: Here's where all of America's aircraft carriers and big-deck amphibious assault ships are as of June 1, 2026. The post Littoral Combat Force Takes Up Station In Caribbean Under Navy’s New Deployment Concept appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Eurosatory ban on Israeli officials may hurt smaller firms, but majors still going to show | Breaking Defense
item.description: Both Elbit Systems and Rafael plan to attend Eurosatory, despite a new ban on Israeli government officials attending.

item.title: 🏛️ Navy unveils the seven companies that will participate in MUSV at-sea testing | Breaking Defense
item.description: Leidos, HII, and Saronic Technologies are among those selected to advance to the at-sea testing phase.

item.title: 📍 The US is struggling with its adversaries over the future of AI. Here’s how to win. | Atlantic Council
item.description: The Atlantic Council's Commission on AI offers a roadmap to harness the transformative potential of the technology while actively managing its risks. The post The US is struggling with its adversaries over the future of AI. Here’s how to win. appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 Turkey’s Secretive Electronic Warfare Jet Appears In New Imagery | The War Zone
item.description: The long-delayed HAVA SOJ is designed to blind enemy air defenses from outside highly contested airspace. The post Turkey’s Secretive Electronic Warfare Jet Appears In New Imagery appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 Northrop Grumman partners with Apex for 2027 SBI demo | Breaking Defense
item.description: Startup Apex Space had previously announced its own self-funded SBI demonstration called Project Shadow by the end of 2026.

item.title: 🌏 South Korea goes full steam ahead on nuclear-powered submarines | Defense News
item.description: Seoul plans to launch its first boat in the mid-2030s and commission it in “the late 2030s,” the government proclaimed.

item.title: 🏴 Building a Better Ukraine Requires Accessibility Reforms | War on the Rocks
item.description: Welcome to The Ukraine Compass, a weekly digest of Ukrainian commentary and analysis from across the political spectrum only for War on the Rocks members. Each Monday, we bring you a curated selection of articles from Ukrainian media offering insight into how Ukrainians…

item.title: 🏛️ Air Force clears T-38s to resume flying | Breaking Defense
item.description: The training jet was grounded May 19; a week after a T-38 crashed in Mississippi.

item.title: 📍 Pakistan’s Diplomatic Pivot Makes It a Trump-Era Power Player | Foreign Policy
item.description: Islamabad is happy to give the U.S. president the image he craves.

item.title: 📍 National security launch schedule not likely impacted by New Glenn disaster | Breaking Defense
item.description: NSSL will be okay because it can still rely on the Falcon 9 workhorse,” said Todd Harrison, senior fellow at the American Enterprise Institute.

item.title: 🕵️ US military seeks cultural advisors in Somalia amid regional strikes | Defense News
item.description: The U.S. special operations task force in Somalia is looking for contractors to advise troops on Somali politics, culture and tribal dynamics.

item.title: 📍 Dangerous Speech in Disguise: The White House’s New “Aliens” Website Is Not a Joke | Just Security
item.description: The White House’s new Aliens.gov website uses fear, dehumanizing rhetoric, and conspiracy themes to build public support for mass deportations. The post Dangerous Speech in Disguise: The White House’s New “Aliens” Website Is Not a Joke appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: ⚛️ Malaysian defense minister publicly slams Norway for cancelled missile sale | Breaking Defense
item.description: “Norway’s action has created more than just a bilateral contract dispute. It raises a deeply troubling question about whether international agreement and strategic partnership can still be trusted at all,” Malaysian Defense Minister Mohamed Khaled Nordin said at the Shangri La…

item.title: 📍 Vietnam’s Quiet Strategy at the Shangri-La Dialogue | The Diplomat
item.description: At the Shangri-La Dialogue, Hanoi has sought to shape the terms of the regional security debate.

item.title: 📍 Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions | Just Security
item.description: A public resource tracking all the legal challenges to the Trump administration's executive orders and actions. The post Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 📍 The 2025 Africa Cup of Nations was an economic success. Here’s how other tournaments can replicate it. | Atlantic Council
item.description: Africa needs a united committee focused on opportunities for sponsorship and investment across multiple sporting events and tournaments. The post The 2025 Africa Cup of Nations was an economic success. Here’s how other tournaments can replicate it. appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 🕵️ The Epidemic of GPS Jamming | Foreign Policy
item.description: Across the world, signals crucial for safe air and sea travel are being disrupted.
